title: Writing - Part 1: Water Conservation
description: Your class has been discussing the global water shortage and what individuals and governments can do to conserve this vital resource.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the measures in your notes. You should explain which measure you believe is more important, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Simple habits such as shorter showers and turning off taps can save significant amounts of water.
Governments must introduce pricing structures and regulations that encourage water saving.
Modern technology can make agriculture and industry far more water-efficient.

structure.intro: Introduce the water conservation challenge and state the two measures you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first measure and its potential effectiveness.
structure.body2: Discuss the second measure and compare it to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which measure you regard as more important and explain your reasoning.

content.modelAnswer:
Access to clean water is one of the most critical issues facing humanity, yet billions of people already live in water-stressed regions. Addressing this challenge requires action at every level. Two important measures are encouraging individual behaviour change and investing in water-saving technology.

Small changes in daily habits can collectively make a significant difference. Using water-efficient appliances, fixing leaks, and reducing consumption during washing and gardening all contribute to conservation. When large numbers of people adopt such habits, the impact can be substantial, particularly in urban areas where water demand is highest.

Investing in technology offers even greater potential. Modern irrigation systems can deliver water directly to plant roots, reducing waste by up to 50 percent compared to traditional methods. Desalination plants and water recycling systems can also expand supply in water-scarce regions.

In my opinion, technological investment is the more important measure. While individual behaviour change is useful, it is inconsistent and difficult to enforce at scale. Technology provides systemic solutions that can be applied across agriculture, industry and cities, addressing water use at the level where consumption is greatest and the savings most significant.
